questions.question: Prove the Basic Proportionality Theorem.
questions.answer: By drawing a line parallel to one side of a triangle and comparing areas of triangles formed, it can be shown that the other two sides are divided in the same ratio.

questions.question: Prove the converse of the Basic Proportionality Theorem.
questions.answer: If a line divides two sides of a triangle in the same ratio, assuming it is not parallel leads to contradiction, proving it must be parallel.

questions.question: Prove the AAA similarity criterion.
questions.answer: When all corresponding angles are equal, sides become proportional, proving similarity.

questions.question: Prove the SSS similarity criterion.
questions.answer: When corresponding sides are proportional, angles become equal, proving triangles are similar.

questions.question: Prove the SAS similarity criterion.
questions.answer: If one angle and including sides are proportional, remaining angles become equal, proving similarity.

questions.question: Derive the relation between areas of similar triangles.
questions.answer: Area ratio equals the square of the ratio of corresponding sides of similar triangles.

questions.question: Explain how similarity helps in proving Pythagoras theorem.
questions.answer: Pythagoras theorem can be derived using proportional sides of similar triangles formed in a right triangle.

questions.question: Prove that medians of similar triangles are proportional.
questions.answer: Since medians scale in the same ratio as sides, they remain proportional in similar triangles.

questions.question: Prove that altitudes of similar triangles are proportional.
questions.answer: Altitudes correspond to sides and hence follow the same scale factor in similar triangles.

questions.question: Explain similarity as a transformation in geometry.
questions.answer: Similarity transforms figures by enlargement or reduction while preserving shape.

questions.question: Explain why similarity criteria are sufficient only for triangles.
questions.answer: Triangles are rigid figures; fixing angles or sides uniquely determines shape.

questions.question: Prove that the ratio of perimeters of similar triangles equals the ratio of their sides.
questions.answer: Since all sides scale equally, the perimeter also scales by the same factor.

questions.question: Explain why similarity preserves angles but not lengths.
questions.answer: Similarity transformations preserve angle measures but change lengths proportionally.

questions.question: Prove that corresponding sides of similar triangles are in the same ratio.
questions.answer: By similarity criteria, proportionality of sides is inherent in similar triangles.

questions.question: Explain why similar triangles are useful in real-life applications.
questions.answer: They help calculate heights, distances, and scales in construction and surveying.

questions.question: Prove that diagonals of a trapezium divide each other proportionally.
questions.answer: Using similarity of triangles formed by diagonals, proportional division is established.

questions.question: Explain why two right triangles with equal acute angles are similar.
questions.answer: Equal acute angles ensure AA similarity, proving triangles are similar.

questions.question: Prove that bisectors of similar triangles are proportional.
questions.answer: Angle bisectors scale in the same ratio as sides in similar triangles.

questions.question: Explain why similarity plays a key role in geometric proofs.
questions.answer: Similarity helps establish proportional relationships essential for proofs.

questions.question: Prove that corresponding heights of similar triangles are proportional.
questions.answer: Heights correspond to sides and hence scale by the same factor.

questions.question: Explain the limitations of similarity in polygons.
questions.answer: Polygons require both angle equality and side proportionality, which is not always sufficient.

questions.question: Explain how similarity is used in architectural drawings.
questions.answer: Architectural drawings use similarity to represent structures on reduced scales.

questions.question: Explain why triangle similarity is central to coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Similarity helps compare slopes, distances, and ratios on coordinate planes.

questions.question: Prove that if two triangles are similar, their corresponding angles are equal.
questions.answer: Angle equality follows directly from the definition of similar triangles.

questions.question: Explain why triangle similarity is considered a unifying concept in geometry.
questions.answer: It connects constructions, proofs, and real-life applications under a single principle.
